load_button:
Load a folder with data files
- only files in this directory will be displayed in the list (no subfolders)
- Standard path: Location of the Script or the .exe file
- keybind: CTRL + O
save_button:
Save the current image or current data
- Image file types: .png, .jpg, .pdf
- Data file types: .dat, .txt, .csv
- Save images in plain format (without the axis): 'Plot settings' -> 'Save plain images'
- image format: 'Plot settings' -> 'Canvas Size'. (maximize app window)
- keybind: CTRL + S
set_button:
Open the settings tab
- Top: Image Plot settings
- Bottom Line Plot settings
- Side: Boolean settings
plot_button:
Reset and reinitialize the plot 
- keybind: CTRL + R
reset_button:
Reset and reinitialize the plot 
- delete all previous multiplots
- keybind: CTRL + R
prev_button:
Plot the previous file in the list
- keybind: CTRL + ←
next_button:
Plot the next file in the list
- keybind: CTRL + →
multiplot_button:
Create multiple plots in one figure 
- choose rows = 1, cols = 1 for single graph multiplots
- Press CTRL+Z to delete the previous plot
- You can click on the subplots to replot them or set the limits
- keybind: CTRL + M
uselabels_button:
Create x,y-labels, add a legend and title
- Legend settings: choose location, add file name to legend
uselims_button:
Choose x,y-limits for line and image plots
- x-limits stay the same when resetting the plot
- y-limits will change to display all figures
- the limits can be set with the sliders or typed in manually
fit_button:
Fit a function to the current data set
- initial fit parameters can be set when the fit does not converge
- use slider to fit only parts of the function
- the textbox can be hidden via the 'Plot settings' -> 'Hide fit params'
normalize_button:
Normalize the plot
- data file: normalize to max = 1 or area = 1 (Plot settings -> normalize Area)
- image file: enhance the contrast of the image (can be changed manually: Plot settings -> pixel range)
- keybind: CTRL + N
lineout_button:
Display a lineout function
- only used for images, no multiplot possible
FFT_button:
Display the |FT(f)|² of the data
- limits of the Fourier window can be set
- zero padding (factor determines number of zeros/x-array length)
- can only be used for line plots, Images don't work.
- if the spectrum is given in nm, the FT will be calculated in fs
data_table_button:
Check this to plot own custom data in the window below
- columns must be separated by a space or tab (not comma or semicolon)
- decimal separator: point and comma works
- You can also plot data by using numpy functions, the argument must be called 'x'.
ent_legend:
Add a Legend to the plot
- Write '{name}' in the textbox if you want to add the file name to the legend.
- The displayed file name can be customized in the legend settings.
- You can access more data information with the following keys:
+ {xmax},{ymax} - x,y coordinate of the maximum point
+ {fwhm} - FWHM around the maximum point
+ {x_res} - x-axis resolution
- For files with multiple y-columns, the legend labels can be set by writing every label into a separate line
- The legend for the second plot of a lineout or a FFT can be accessed with the second line of the legend entry
ent_subplot:
Specify the number of sub plots in each multiplot window
- Not used for plotting in a single window (rows=1, cols=1)
two_axis_button:
Initiate a second y-axis on the right
- The x-axis is assumed to be the same
- The button is reset to 'off' when a new multiplot window is created
legend_settings_button:
- Set the location of the legend
- Customize the display of {name} by using a sub-string of the file name
update_labels_button:
- Update the labels and titles
 - To update the legend, select the line by clicking on it (hide the line)
choose_ax_button: Choose the axis, along which the lineout point will move
save_lineout_button: Save the lineout into a data file
yerror_area_button:
off: Use errorbars with caps
on: Display the y-error as a shaded area above and below the line
mid_axis_button:
off: Use standard outside axis
on: Use middle line axis centered at (0,0)
function_entry:
Enter a mathematical function to display, Use numpy syntax
 List of possible functions:
- np.sin(x), np.cos(x), np.tan(x)
- np.arcsin(x), np.arccos(x)
- np.deg2rad(x), np.rad2deg(x)
- np.sinh(x), np.cosh(x), np.tanh(x)
- np.exp(x), np.log(x), np.log10(x), np.log2(x)
- np.i0(x) - Bessel function, np.sinc(x)
- np.sqrt(x), np.cbrt(x), np.sign(x)
- np.heaviside(x,0)
subfolder_button: Display also files from any subfolder within the current directory
settings_canvas_ratio_list:
Determine the size of the canvas. This is useful for saving the plots in a reproducible manner
- Auto: Resize the canvas to fill the full screen (standard)
- Custom: Enter the width and height manually using the two entry boxes on the right
- 4:3 ratio: Set the size of the canvas in 4:3 format, the width [cm] can be set with the entry box on the right
settings_canvas_width:
Set the width of the canvas in cm.
- Works only, if Canvas Size is not set to 'Auto'
settings_pixel_size:
Set the size of a single pixel
- the tick labels can be converted to real lengths by checking 'Convert Pixels'.
settings_label_dist: Set, at which points a new label is placed
settings_aspect:
Set the aspect of the image
- 'equal' - original image aspect, no pixel distortion
- 'auto' - fill the image to the size of the window, produces image distortion.
settings_clim_slider:
Enhance the contrast of the images by selecting the pixel range
- Uncheck 'Normalize' for manual setting
settings_plot_type_list:
Choose between different plotting scales
- Normal: plt.plot(...)
- Semi Logarithmic x: plt.semilogx(...)
- Semi Logarithmic y: plt.semilogy(...)
- Log-Log plot: plt.loglog(...)
settings_linestyle_list: Choose different line styles for the plot.
settings_marker_list: Determine, if every data point is highlighted with a marker. Different marker shapes can be chosen.
settings_cmap_list:
Choose between different color cycles for multiplots. We differentiate between qualtitative and sequential colormaps:
- Qualitative Cmap: We cycle through a list of predefined colors of specific length
- CUSTOM: Use a single color. The color can be chosen by the dropdown menu on the right
- Sequential Cmap: We create a list of colors based on a continuous colormap. The number of colors can be set by the dropdown menu on the right
settings_grid_lines_list:
Check 'Use Grid'
- Decide between showing major (and minor) grid lines.
settings_grid_axis_list:
Check 'Use Grid'
- Decide between showing only x- oder y-grid lines
settings_moving_av_list:
Perform a moving average between neighbouring points to smooth the data.
- set the number of neighbouring points
- n = 1: no moving average
- n=2: left and right neighbouring point
settings_convert_pixels_button: Convert the integer pixel labels to lengths in [mm] by stating the 'pixel size'
settings_scale_switch_button:
Show a scalebar in the bottom right corner.
- Scale determined by 'pixel size'
- Recommendation: use this setting together with 'hide ticks'
settings_cbar_switch_button: Show a colorbar on the right to attribute the color to the numeric 'gray-value' between 0 and 1.
settings_save_plain_img_button:
When saving a figure with 'Save Figure', only the picture will be saved in the raw format.
Note: The colormap and current size of the image will be saved.
settings_hide_params_button: When using a fit function, the textbox with the fit parameters in the plot will be hidden.
settings_normalize_list:
Only for line plots:
- Maximum: Normalize to a maximum value of 1
- Area: Normalize to area of 1
- Only Factor: Do not normalize, just multiply the data with the factor
